page: Microsoft Azure M32-8ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz and includes a 38 MB L3 cache. The standout hardware characteristic is its massive memory capacity of 875.0 GB, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 109.38 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 1049 GB volume, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data reveals a significant performance asymmetry. While the memory capacity is massive, the compute capabilities are constrained. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests rank in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory read bandwidth for both small and large blocks is weak, falling in the bottom 25%, though large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance are weak, reflecting the limited processing throughput of the 8 vCPUs. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks also register poor performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M32-8ms offers high resource density specifically for memory capacity, making it suitable for workloads where RAM footprint is the primary constraint rather than raw compute power. However, the tradeoff is a low compute-per-resource ratio. This makes the server appropriate for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications with massive memory requirements but low parallel processing demands. It is less suitable for compute-heavy tasks like software compilation or intensive data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure M32-8ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 875.0 GB, translating to 109.38 GB of RAM per core, alongside 1049 GB of local storage. While the memory allocation is massive, benchmark results indicate limited compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory read bandwidth is also weak, though large block write bandwidth achieves average marks. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32-8ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 875 GB of RAM, and 1049 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32-8ms is a memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 875 GB RAM, and 1049 GB storage, built for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high-density memory configuration featuring massive RAM capacity paired with dedicated compute resources for specialized memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 38 MB L3 cache
Massive 875.0 GB RAM capacity providing 109.38 GB per core
1049 GB local storage with Premium Storage support
Best suited for large in-memory databases and caching layers
